selected_cards.signal_priority_card: Added dwell-time based P(skip) to reduce skipped updates by downranking high-skip posts.
selected_cards.operator_implication: Treat this as a recent platform adjustment; monitor for recency drift and keep claims tied to source_ref.

selected_cards.signal_priority_card: First-pass candidate generation followed by ML second-pass ranking.
selected_cards.operator_implication: Design hooks and structure so content survives candidate selection before heavier ranking stages.

selected_cards.signal_priority_card: Ranking uses user interactions, video info, and device/account settings with different weights.
selected_cards.operator_implication: Optimize for the strong signals explicitly named in the source, not generic engagement folklore.

selected_cards.signal_priority_card: Added Manage Topics and Smart Keyword Filters to give users more control over recommendations.
selected_cards.operator_implication: Treat this as a recent platform adjustment; monitor for recency drift and keep claims tied to source_ref.

selected_cards.signal_priority_card: A significant portion of feed content is AI-recommended from accounts a user does not follow (>20%).
selected_cards.operator_implication: Treat this as a recent platform adjustment; monitor for recency drift and keep claims tied to source_ref.

selected_cards.signal_priority_card: Explore uses multi-stage retrieval and ranking funnel; Two-tower retrieval used for candidate sourcing.
selected_cards.operator_implication: Design hooks and structure so content survives candidate selection before heavier ranking stages.

selected_cards.signal_priority_card: Three-stage pipeline: candidate sourcing, ML ranking, heuristics/filters; served by Home Mixer.
selected_cards.operator_implication: Design hooks and structure so content survives candidate selection before heavier ranking stages.

selected_cards.signal_priority_card: Ranking uses a ~48M parameter neural network trained on interaction labels.
selected_cards.operator_implication: Assume the system generalizes across sparse user/content features; consistency and metadata quality matter.
